 {"id":"bd-3s2f","title":"EPIC: Webhook System for External Integrations","description":"## Overview
Implement an event-driven webhook system that triggers HTTP callbacks on NTM events, enabling integration with Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, CI/CD systems, and custom automation.

## Problem Statement
NTM operates as a closed system. Users can't:
- Get notified in Slack when agents complete work
- Trigger CI pipelines when all agents finish
- Alert PagerDuty when errors occur
- Build custom dashboards from live data
- Integrate with existing DevOps tooling

## Key Features
1. Configurable webhook endpoints per event type
2. Built-in formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams
3. Retry logic with exponential backoff
4. Event filtering by session, agent type, severity
5. Payload templates with variable substitution

## Event Types
- agent.started: Agent session created
- agent.completed: Agent finished task
- agent.error: Agent encountered error
- agent.idle: Agent became idle
- session.created: New NTM session
- session.ended: Session terminated
- bead.assigned: Bead assigned to agent
- bead.completed: Bead marked done

## Configuration (ntm.yaml)
webhooks:
  - url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
    events: [agent.completed, agent.error]
    formatter: slack
    filter:
      session: myproject*
      severity: [warning, error, critical]
  - url: https://my-api.com/ntm-events
    events: [*]
    formatter: json

## Success Criteria
- Webhooks fire reliably on configured events
- Built-in formatters produce valid Slack/Discord messages
- Failures don't block NTM operation
- Easy to configure without code changes

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/webhook/manager.go (new - webhook orchestration)
- internal/webhook/formatters/ (new - Slack, Discord, JSON formatters)
- internal/config/webhooks.go (new - webhook config parsing)
- internal/events/emitter.go (event emission points)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3s2wn","title":"Task: tmux pipe-pane streaming integration","description":"## Scope\nStream live pane output using tmux pipe-pane with fallback capture-pane polling.\n\n## Implementation\n- On pane spawn: tmux pipe-pane -o to internal streamer.\n- Streamer emits WS events + writes to log for replay.\n- Fallback to capture-pane polling if pipe-pane fails.\n\n## Logging\n- Log pipe-pane attach/detach, fallback activation, pane IDs.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: streamer handles chunk boundaries and encoding.\n- Integration: spawn session, verify WS output matches pane content.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Live output latency < 100ms under normal load.\n- Fallback works when pipe-pane unavailable.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:51:44.010812813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:31:28.933982268Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:31:28.933849621Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3s2wn","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3s2wn","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3scdq","title":"E2E Tests: Encryption-at-rest for prompt history + event logs","description":"## Goal
End-to-end validation that when encryption is enabled, on-disk NTM artifacts are not plaintext.

## Scenario
1. Enable encryption (via env/config as per bd-2euwg).
2. Generate a prompt + event that would normally write to disk.
3. Verify:
   - files exist
   - raw file bytes do **not** contain plaintext substrings (search for known synthetic marker)
4. Verify NTM can still display/decrypt correctly via its own commands.

## Test Harness + Isolation
- Implement as Go E2E tests under `e2e/` with the existing build tag (`//go:build e2e`).
- Tests must be hermetic:
  - set `HOME` and/or `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to `t.TempDir()` so artifacts are written only into temp dirs
  - never read real user keys/config

## Logging
- Use `e2e.TestLogger` (writes to `E2E_LOG_DIR` or `/tmp/ntm-e2e-logs`).
- Emit a `test_run_id` and log:
  - config used (redacted)
  - file paths created
  - size + sha256 (of ciphertext)
  - grep results for plaintext marker

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default unit suite remains green: `go test ./...`.
- E2E suite passes: `go test -tags e2e ./e2e`.
- Never uses real keys; only synthetic fixtures.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:56.210578636Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T04:11:57.449990143Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3scdq","depends_on_id":"bd-29cp6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:56.217019163Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-3scdq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld77","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:56.224249431Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3sha","title":"Task: Dashboard help respects minimal/full verbosity","description":"## Overview
Replace tier‑based dashboard hints with the **help verbosity** model (minimal/full) and optional debug mode, aligning with CLI help simplification.

## Deliverables
1. Update internal/tui/dashboard/help.go:
   - Minimal: essential keys only (navigate/select/quit)
   - Full: all standard keys (refresh/zoom/etc.)
   - Debug: add debug keys/panels when `--debug` or `NTM_DEBUG=1`

2. Status bar hint:
   - Show current help verbosity: `Help: minimal` or `Help: full`

3. Panel visibility:
   - Minimal: core panels (activity/status)
   - Full: all standard panels
   - Debug: include internal panels if available

## Implementation Notes
- Read verbosity from config at dashboard init
- Cache to avoid repeated file reads
- Update hints when verbosity changes (rare)

## Tests
- Unit: mapping of verbosity → key list
- E2E: dashboard rendering in minimal/full/debug with logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard respects help verbosity setting
- Keybinding hints filter appropriately
- Verbosity indicator shows in status bar
- E2E tests for minimal/full/debug rendering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteTower","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:38.988102962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:24:27.821147939Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:24:27.821083920Z","close_reason":"Implemented dashboard help verbosity (minimal/full) + debug gating + status indicator; added/updated tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3sha","depends_on_id":"bd-352n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3sha","depends_on_id":"bd-vcmnv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3sl0s","title":"Task: Apply redaction before persisting prompt history + event logs","description":"## Scope
Ensure secrets/PII are not written to disk in:
- prompt history
- event log JSONL
- output archives (where feasible)

## Notes
- Event logs should store redaction summaries (counts/categories), not raw secrets.
- Prompt history may need two variants:
  - original prompt (in-memory only)
  - persisted prompt (redacted)

## Acceptance Criteria
- With redaction enabled, searching local logs cannot recover raw secret substrings.
- Unit tests cover at least one log write path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:11.149236236Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:11.170007235Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3sl0s","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:11.161595114Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-3sl0s","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:11.169988971Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3sl6a","title":"Task: Write E2E test for observability metrics","description":"E2E tests for coverage map, redundancy score, velocity metrics, and conflict density.

## Test Harness
- Use deterministic fixtures (no real model calls)
- Feed synthetic ModeOutput + token counts through metrics pipeline
- Run via existing e2e suite in `e2e/`

## Test Cases

### Coverage Map
```go
// e2e/ensemble_metrics_test.go
func TestE2E_Metrics_CoverageMap(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: fixed mode assignments with known categories
    // Verify: Coverage map shows correct category coverage
    // Verify: Blind spots correctly identified
}
```

### Redundancy Score
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_RedundancyScore_LowRedundancy(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: outputs with disjoint findings
    // Verify: Low redundancy score
}

func TestE2E_Metrics_RedundancyScore_HighRedundancy(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: overlapping findings + evidence pointers
    // Verify: High redundancy score + suggestions
}
```

### Findings Velocity
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_FindingsVelocity(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: outputs with known findings + token counts
    // Verify: Velocity per mode + overall
    // Verify: Underperformers flagged
}
```

### Conflict Density
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_ConflictDensity(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: conflict list from AuditReport (preferred) + fallback heuristic
    // Verify: Conflicts detected and counted
    // Verify: High-conflict pairs identified
}
```

### Post-Run Report
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_PostRunReport(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: complete ensemble run with metrics summary
    // Verify: Report includes all metrics + suggestions
}
```

## Logging
- t.Logf metric inputs (mode IDs, tokens, findings counts)
- t.Logf computed metric values
- Log threshold comparisons and selected suggestions

 {"id":"bd-f8ikz","title":"Task: REST account management endpoints","description":"## Scope\nExpose CAAM account management via REST.\n\n## Endpoints\n- /accounts, /accounts/{id}, /accounts/{id}/rotate, /accounts/active, /accounts/quota, /accounts/auto-rotate, /accounts/history.\n\n## Logging\n- Log rotation events, account IDs, and quota snapshots.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: validation + error codes.\n- Integration: rotate account and verify active/quota updates.\n- WebSocket: account.rotated events.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Account rotation and status queries work via REST.\n- Errors match robot mode codes.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TopazCliff","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:51:04.038607736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T21:22:24.767699385Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T21:22:24.767636428Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-f8ikz","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-fgnlw","title":"E2E Tests: support-bundle generation + safe redaction","description":"## Goal
Validate the full CLI + robot flow for generating a support bundle.

## Scenarios
1. No session running:
   - bundle still includes doctor + versions + config (effective)
2. With a running session:
   - include a bounded pane excerpt
   - ensure redaction placeholders appear instead of raw secrets
3. Privacy mode session:
   - verify pane content inclusion is refused unless explicit override

## Test Harness + Isolation
- Implement as Go E2E tests under `e2e/` with the existing build tag (`//go:build e2e`).
- Tests must be hermetic:
  - set `HOME` and/or `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to `t.TempDir()` so bundle inputs (config/logs) are controlled
  - avoid reading/modifying real user artifacts

## Logging Requirements
- Use `e2e.TestLogger` (writes to `E2E_LOG_DIR` or `/tmp/ntm-e2e-logs`).
- Structured per-test logging with:
  - `test_run_id`
  - output archive path
  - manifest path + schema version
- Log:
  - command lines
  - stdout/stderr
  - parsed manifest JSON (pretty printed)
  - file list with sizes + sha256
  - redaction summaries per file

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default unit suite remains green: `go test ./...`.
- E2E suite passes: `go test -tags e2e ./e2e`.
- Tests verify archive layout and manifest checksums.
- Tests assert plaintext secret markers are absent from archived text files when redaction is enabled.
- Logs include stdout/stderr + paths created.

## Bonus Coverage (recommended)
- Also exercise the robot surface once `--robot-support-bundle` exists:
  - parse JSON output and assert stable fields (`success`, `timestamp`, `path`, summary fields).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:28:36.051004653Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T03:15:50.096525014Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fgnlw","depends_on_id":"bd-1a542","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:28:36.073192430Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-fgnlw","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi1i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:15:23.401563044Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fgnlw","depends_on_id":"bd-5h1bi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:28:36.063986788Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fgt43","title":"Unit tests: internal/git (2.0% coverage)","description":"Current: 2.0% coverage. Package provides git operations (branch parsing, diff extraction, status parsing, commit message formatting). Target: test all pure string-processing helpers without touching the filesystem. Functions to investigate: status line parsers, branch name extractors, diff stat formatters, commit message builders. Approach: read git.go and identify all functions that take string/struct input and return string/struct output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T15:50:39.013497729Z","created_by":"BrightPeak","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:50:42.780985166Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:50:42.780889868Z","close_reason":"Package is 98% exec.Command calls (git CLI). Only 1 pure function (NewWorktreeService constructor). No meaningful pure-logic test coverage possible.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fgt43","depends_on_id":"bd-1a5a5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":53,"issue_id":"bd-fgt43","author":"BrightPeak","text":"ANALYSIS: Only 1 pure-logic function found: parseWorktreeList (worktree.go:271-327) - parses git worktree list --porcelain output. All other functions (FindProjectRoot, IsGitRepository, getCurrentBranch, getCommitHash, etc.) call exec.Command and are not testable without mocks. Coverage ceiling without mocks is very low (~5-10%). Consider reclassifying as I/O-heavy package.","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:04:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fj9f","title":"Task: Implement contextual command suggestions","description":"## Overview
Provide intelligent, context-aware command suggestions based on current NTM state.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/suggestions/contextual.go:
   - SuggestNextCommand(state State) Suggestion
   - State struct capturing current NTM status
   - Suggestion struct with command, reason, example

2. Context detection:
   - No sessions running -> suggest spawn
   - Sessions running, none busy -> suggest send
   - Sessions running, all busy -> suggest status or dashboard
   - Recent errors -> suggest tail or logs
   - Many sessions -> suggest dashboard
   - Beads available -> suggest assign or swarm

3. Suggestion display:
   - Show at end of command output (non-intrusive)
   - Format: 'Next: ntm send myproject --msg="Start task"'
   - Make dismissible via config (suggestions_enabled: false)

4. Integration points:
   - After ntm status: suggest send if agents idle
   - After ntm spawn: suggest send
   - After ntm send: suggest status or tail
   - After error: suggest relevant recovery command

## Implementation Notes
- Keep suggestions brief (one line)
- Don't show suggestion if user seems to know what they're doing
- Rate limit to avoid being annoying

## Acceptance Criteria
- Suggestions appear in appropriate contexts
- Suggestions are actionable and relevant
- Can be disabled via config
- Unit tests for each context scenario","notes":"Optional, non-intrusive suggestions.  {"id":"bd-fv2hy","title":"Task: Implement real-time synthesis streaming","description":"## Implementation
Add streaming synthesis without altering non-stream behavior:

1. Add `SynthesisChunk` + stream method in existing synthesis code (prefer extending `internal/ensemble/synthesis.go`; only add a new file if unavoidable):
   - `StreamSynthesize(ctx, inputs) (<-chan SynthesisChunk, <-chan error)`
   - Chunk fields: type, content, mode_id, index, timestamp

2. Emission rules:
   - Deterministic ordering (monotonic index)
   - Progress/status chunks at key milestones
   - Backpressure-safe channel usage

3. CLI:
   - `ntm ensemble synthesize --stream` (human-readable incremental output)
   - `--stream --json` emits JSONL events + final summary
   - Ctrl+C cancels, writes checkpoint, prints resume command

4. Checkpoint integration:
   - On cancel/error, write partial checkpoint (bd-3didt)
   - On resume, continue emitting chunks from last index

## Logging
- Log stream start/end, chunk_count, time_to_first_chunk, cancel reason

## Tests
- Unit: chunk ordering + deterministic emission
- Integration: stream + synthesis fixture
- E2E: CLI streaming output and resume path

## Dependencies
- Synthesis System (bd-2qwm8)
- Synthesis strategies (bd-2ztw9)
- Partial recovery (bd-3didt)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:42:12.555031671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:55:25.570334908Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:55:25.570260119Z","close_reason":"Completed (streaming synth + checkpoints + E2E tests)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2nnsg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-3didt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

+//go:build e2e
+// +build e2e
+
+// Package e2e contains end-to-end tests for NTM robot mode commands.
+// [E2E-PREFLIGHT] Tests for prompt preflight validation: secret detection, destructive commands.
+package e2e
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"os"
+	"os/exec"
+	"strings"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+)
+
+// Synthetic test fixtures for preflight detection (never use real keys).
+// These patterns match the internal/lint and internal/redaction detection rules.
+const (
+	// Anthropic API key format: sk-ant-api03-[50+ chars]
+	preflightFakeAnthropicKey = "sk-ant-api03-FAKEtestkey12345678901234567890123456789012345678901234"
+	// OpenAI project key format: sk-proj-[40+ chars]
+	preflightFakeOpenAIKey = "sk-proj-FAKEtestkey1234567890123456789012345678901234"
+	// GitHub personal token format: ghp_[30+ chars]
+	preflightFakeGitHubToken = "ghp_FAKEtesttokenvalue12345678901234567"
+	// AWS Access Key format: AKIA[16 chars]
+	preflightFakeAWSKey = "AKIAFAKETEST12345678"
+	// JWT format: eyJ[base64].eyJ[base64].[signature]
+	preflightFakeJWT = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.SflKxwRJSMeKKF2QT4fwpMeJf36POk6yJV_adQssw5c"
+)
+
+// PreflightResult represents the JSON output from ntm preflight --json.
+type PreflightResult struct {
+	Success         bool               `json:"success"`
+	Timestamp       string             `json:"timestamp"`
+	Version         string             `json:"version"`
+	PreviewHash     string             `json:"preview_hash"`
+	PreviewLen      int                `json:"preview_len"`
+	EstimatedTokens int                `json:"estimated_tokens"`
+	Findings        []PreflightFinding `json:"findings"`
+	ErrorCount      int                `json:"error_count"`
+	WarningCount    int                `json:"warning_count"`
+	InfoCount       int                `json:"info_count"`
+	Preview         string             `json:"preview,omitempty"`
+	DCGAvailable    bool               `json:"dcg_available"`
+	Error           string             `json:"error,omitempty"`
+	ErrorCode       string             `json:"error_code,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// PreflightFinding represents a single finding from preflight.
+type PreflightFinding struct {
+	ID       string                 `json:"id"`
+	Severity string                 `json:"severity"`
+	Message  string                 `json:"message"`
+	Help     string                 `json:"help"`
+	Metadata map[string]interface{} `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
+	Start    int                    `json:"start,omitempty"`
+	End      int                    `json:"end,omitempty"`
+	Line     int                    `json:"line,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// PreflightTestSuite manages E2E tests for preflight functionality.
+type PreflightTestSuite struct {
+	t       *testing.T
+	logger  *TestLogger
+	tempDir string
+	cleanup []func()
+}
+
+// NewPreflightTestSuite creates a new preflight test suite.
+func NewPreflightTestSuite(t *testing.T, scenario string) *PreflightTestSuite {
+	t.Helper()
+	SkipIfNoNTM(t)
+
+	tempDir := t.TempDir()
+	logger := NewTestLogger(t, "preflight-"+scenario)
+
+	suite := &PreflightTestSuite{
+		t:       t,
+		logger:  logger,
+		tempDir: tempDir,
+		cleanup: make([]func(), 0),
+	}
+
+	t.Cleanup(func() {
+		logger.Close()
+		for _, fn := range suite.cleanup {
+			fn()
+		}
+	})
+
+	return suite
+}
+
+// runPreflight runs the preflight command and returns parsed results.
+func (s *PreflightTestSuite) runPreflight(prompt string, strict bool) (*PreflightResult, string, string, error) {
+	args := []string{"preflight", prompt, "--json"}
+	if strict {
+		args = append(args, "--strict")
+	}
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Running: ntm %s", strings.Join(args, " "))
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Prompt (truncated): %s", truncateForLog(prompt, 100))
+
+	cmd := exec.Command("ntm", args...)
+	cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "HOME="+s.tempDir)
+
+	var stdout, stderr strings.Builder
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
+
+	err := cmd.Run()
+
+	stdoutStr := stdout.String()
+	stderrStr := stderr.String()
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] stdout: %s", stdoutStr)
+	if stderrStr != "" {
+		s.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] stderr: %s", stderrStr)
+	}
+	if err != nil {
+		s.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] error: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Parse JSON output
+	var result PreflightResult
+	if jsonErr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(stdoutStr), &result); jsonErr != nil {
+		s.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] JSON parse error: %v", jsonErr)
+		return nil, stdoutStr, stderrStr, jsonErr
+	}
+
+	s.logger.LogJSON("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Result", result)
+	return &result, stdoutStr, stderrStr, err
+}
+
+// findingByID returns the first finding with the given ID.
+func (s *PreflightTestSuite) findingByID(result *PreflightResult, id string) *PreflightFinding {
+	for _, f := range result.Findings {
+		if f.ID == id {
+			return &f
+		}
+	}
+	return nil
+}
+
+// hasAnyFindingWithID returns true if any finding has the given ID.
+func (s *PreflightTestSuite) hasAnyFindingWithID(result *PreflightResult, id string) bool {
+	return s.findingByID(result, id) != nil
+}
+
+// truncateForLog truncates a string for logging purposes.
+func truncateForLog(s string, n int) string {
+	if len(s) <= n {
+		return s
+	}
+	return s[:n] + "..."
+}
+
+// TestPreflightSecretDetection_AnthropicKey tests detection of Anthropic API keys.
+func TestPreflightSecretDetection_AnthropicKey(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "secret-anthropic")
+
+	prompt := "Please use this API key: " + preflightFakeAnthropicKey + " for authentication."
+
+	result, stdout, stderr, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: Anthropic key detection")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: secret-anthropic-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] command: ntm preflight <prompt> --json")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stdout: %s", stdout)
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stderr: %s", stderr)
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify secret was detected
+	if !suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "secret_detected") {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected secret_detected finding for Anthropic key")
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] findings: %+v", result.Findings)
+	}
+
+	// Verify warning count
+	if result.WarningCount == 0 && result.ErrorCount == 0 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected at least 1 warning or error for secret")
+	}
+
+	// Verify robot output structure
+	if result.PreviewHash == "" {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected non-empty preview_hash")
+	}
+	if result.PreviewLen == 0 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected non-zero preview_len")
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] secret_detection_test_completed: anthropic_key")
+}
+
+// TestPreflightSecretDetection_GitHubToken tests detection of GitHub tokens.
+func TestPreflightSecretDetection_GitHubToken(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "secret-github")
+
+	prompt := "Token: " + preflightFakeGitHubToken + " - use for auth"
+
+	result, _, _, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: GitHub token detection")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: secret-github-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify secret was detected
+	if !suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "secret_detected") {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected secret_detected finding for GitHub token")
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] findings: %+v", result.Findings)
+	}
+
+	// Check category metadata if available
+	finding := suite.findingByID(result, "secret_detected")
+	if finding != nil && finding.Metadata != nil {
+		if cat, ok := finding.Metadata["category"].(string); ok {
+			suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] detected_category: %s", cat)
+		}
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] secret_detection_test_completed: github_token")
+}
+

+// TestPreflightDestructive_RmRf tests detection of rm -rf commands.
+func TestPreflightDestructive_RmRf(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "destructive-rmrf")
+
+	prompt := "To clean up, run rm -rf / to delete everything"
+
+	result, stdout, stderr, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: rm -rf detection")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: destructive-rmrf-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] command: ntm preflight <prompt> --json")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stdout: %s", stdout)
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stderr: %s", stderr)
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify destructive command was detected
+	if !suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "destructive_command") {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected destructive_command finding for rm -rf /")
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] findings: %+v", result.Findings)
+	}
+
+	// In default mode, destructive should be warning (not error)
+	finding := suite.findingByID(result, "destructive_command")
+	if finding != nil {
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] finding_severity: %s", finding.Severity)
+		if finding.Severity != "warning" {
+			t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected warning severity in default mode, got %s", finding.Severity)
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Success should be true in default mode (warnings don't block)
+	if !result.Success {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected success=true in default mode (warnings don't block)")
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] destructive_test_completed: rm_rf")
+}
+

+// TestPreflightStrictMode_BlocksDestructive tests that strict mode escalates warnings to errors.
+func TestPreflightStrictMode_BlocksDestructive(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "strict-mode")
+
+	prompt := "Run rm -rf /tmp/important to clean cache"
+
+	result, stdout, stderr, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, true)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: strict mode blocks destructive")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: strict-mode-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] command: ntm preflight <prompt> --json --strict")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stdout: %s", stdout)
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stderr: %s", stderr)
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify destructive command was detected with error severity
+	finding := suite.findingByID(result, "destructive_command")
+	if finding == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected destructive_command finding")
+	}
+
+	if finding.Severity != "error" {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected error severity in strict mode, got %s", finding.Severity)
+	}
+
+	// Success should be false when there are errors
+	if result.Success {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected success=false in strict mode with errors")
+	}
+
+	// Error code should be set
+	if result.ErrorCode == "" {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected error_code when blocking")
+	}
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] error_code: %s", result.ErrorCode)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] strict_mode_test_completed")
+}

+// TestPreflightBenignPrompt tests that benign prompts pass without findings.
+func TestPreflightBenignPrompt(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "benign")
+
+	prompt := "Please fix the bug in the auth.go file where the login function fails on timeout."
+
+	result, _, _, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: benign prompt")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: benign-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify no concerning findings
+	if suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "secret_detected") {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Unexpected secret_detected for benign prompt")
+	}
+	if suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "destructive_command") {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Unexpected destructive_command for benign prompt")
+	}
+
+	// Success should be true
+	if !result.Success {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected success=true for benign prompt")
+	}
+
+	// ErrorCount should be 0
+	if result.ErrorCount != 0 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected error_count=0, got %d", result.ErrorCount)
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] benign_prompt_test_completed")
+}
+
+// TestPreflightSafePatterns tests that safe patterns are not flagged.
+func TestPreflightSafePatterns(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "safe-patterns")
+
+	// These are safe patterns that should NOT trigger warnings
+	safePrompts := []struct {
+		name   string
+		prompt string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:   "git_force_with_lease",
+			prompt: "Push your changes with git push --force-with-lease origin feature",
+		},
+		{
+			name:   "rm_node_modules",
+			prompt: "Clean up by running rm -rf node_modules and reinstall",
+		},
+		{
+			name:   "rm_dist",
+			prompt: "Delete the build output with rm -rf dist/",
+		},
+		{
+			name:   "git_soft_reset",
+			prompt: "Undo the last commit with git reset --soft HEAD~1",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range safePrompts {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			result, _, _, err := suite.runPreflight(tc.prompt, false)
+
+			suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Testing safe pattern: %s", tc.name)
+
+			if result == nil {
+				t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+			}
+
+			// Verify destructive command was NOT detected
+			if suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "destructive_command") {
+				t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Unexpected destructive_command for safe pattern: %s", tc.name)
+				suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] findings: %+v", result.Findings)
+			}
+
+			suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] safe_pattern_test_completed: %s", tc.name)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// TestPreflightRobotOutput tests the robot output format stability.
+func TestPreflightRobotOutput(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "robot-output")
+
+	prompt := "Test prompt for robot output validation"
+
+	result, stdout, _, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: robot output format")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: robot-output-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] raw_json: %s", stdout)
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify required robot fields
+	if result.Timestamp == "" {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Missing timestamp field")
+	}
+	if result.Version == "" {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Missing version field")
+	}
+	if result.PreviewHash == "" {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Missing preview_hash field")
+	}
+	if result.PreviewLen == 0 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Missing or zero preview_len field")
+	}
+	if result.Findings == nil {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Missing findings field (should be empty array, not null)")
+	}
+
+	// Verify JSON is valid and parseable
+	var rawJSON map[string]interface{}
+	if parseErr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(stdout), &rawJSON); parseErr != nil {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Output is not valid JSON: %v", parseErr)
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.LogJSON("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Parsed robot output", rawJSON)
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] robot_output_test_completed")
+}
+
+// TestPreflightHashStability tests that preview hash is deterministic.
+func TestPreflightHashStability(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "hash-stability")
+
+	prompt := "Identical prompt for hash stability test"
+
+	// Run preflight multiple times
+	var hashes []string
+	for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
+		result, _, _, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+		if result == nil {
+			t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result on iteration %d: %v", i, err)
+		}
+		hashes = append(hashes, result.PreviewHash)
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Iteration %d hash: %s", i, result.PreviewHash)
+	}
+
+	// All hashes should be identical
+	for i := 1; i < len(hashes); i++ {
+		if hashes[i] != hashes[0] {
+			t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Hash mismatch: iteration 0 = %s, iteration %d = %s", hashes[0], i, hashes[i])
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Different prompt should have different hash
+	differentResult, _, _, _ := suite.runPreflight("A completely different prompt", false)
+	if differentResult != nil && differentResult.PreviewHash == hashes[0] {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Different prompts should have different hashes")
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] hash_stability_test_completed")
+}
+
+// TestPreflightMultipleFindings tests handling of prompts with multiple issues.
+func TestPreflightMultipleFindings(t *testing.T) {
+	suite := NewPreflightTestSuite(t, "multiple-findings")
+
+	// Prompt with both a secret AND a destructive command
+	prompt := "Use API key " + preflightFakeOpenAIKey + " then run rm -rf / to clean up"
+
+	result, stdout, stderr, err := suite.runPreflight(prompt, false)
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Test: multiple findings")
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] test_run_id: multiple-findings-%d", time.Now().Unix())
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stdout: %s", stdout)
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] full_stderr: %s", stderr)
+
+	if result == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Failed to parse result: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Should have at least 2 findings
+	if len(result.Findings) < 2 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected at least 2 findings, got %d", len(result.Findings))
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] findings: %+v", result.Findings)
+	}
+
+	// Should have both secret and destructive findings
+	hasSecret := suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "secret_detected")
+	hasDestructive := suite.hasAnyFindingWithID(result, "destructive_command")
+
+	if !hasSecret {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected secret_detected finding")
+	}
+	if !hasDestructive {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Expected destructive_command finding")
+	}
+
+	// Log all findings
+	for i, f := range result.Findings {
+		suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] Finding %d: id=%s severity=%s message=%s",
+			i, f.ID, f.Severity, f.Message)
+	}
+
+	suite.logger.Log("[E2E-PREFLIGHT] multiple_findings_test_completed")
+}
